summary: Large decompilation pass for the Name Entry menu unit `main/melee/mn/mnnamenew`, replacing many `/// #` stubs with C and adding supporting type/prototype work. The decomp.dev bot reported 5 new full matches totaling +1712 bytes and 20 improved unmatched items, with the biggest gains in `mnNameNew_MainInput`, `mnNameNew_KeySetup`, `mnNameNew_GlyphVariantSetup`, and related name-entry helpers. The PR also split a large anonymous data label in `symbols.txt` into individual `mnNameNew_803EE35C`-style string symbols, added `NameNewEntry` and `GlyphVariantEntry` structs, and tightened `HSD_SisLib_803A74F0` from `u8*` color data to `GXColor*`, causing call-site cast cleanup across result/menu/item/library code. Evidence for reviewer rationale is weak: the human PR text only says "Tracking PR as usual" and there were no review comments.
what_changed: Implemented substantial portions of `src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.c`, expanding the file from a mostly stubbed unit to 1600+ lines of C for name-entry UI behavior.

Newly implemented or improved functions include `mnNameNew_8023B0F8`, `mnNameNew_8023B314`, `mnNameNew_KeySetup`, `mnNameNew_8023BAA8`, `PickAutoName`, `NameContainsOnlySpaces`, `WriteCharactersForNameAtIndex`, `AddCharacterToName`, `mnNameNew_GlyphVariantInput`, `mnNameNew_MainInput`, `mnNameNew_8023CE4C`, `fn_8023CFC8`, `mnNameNew_GlyphVariantSetup`, `InitNameEntryUIState`, and related helpers.

Added name-entry UI data structures to `src/melee/mn/types.h`: `GlyphVariantEntry` with JObj array and text pointer, and `NameNewEntry` with mode, cursor, name index, auto-name history, key/name/description text, and variant gobj fields.
Updated `src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.h` from many `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations to typed prototypes using `NameNewEntry*`, `GlyphVariantEntry*`, `HSD_GObj*`, `HSD_Archive*`, `u8`, `s32`, and `bool`.

Split `lbl_803EE35C` in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` into many individual `mnNameNew_803EE35C` through `mnNameNew_803EE708` string symbols, including strings such as `mnnamenew.c`, `user_data`, `MenMainBack_Top_joint`, `mnNameAutoNameUs`, and `mnNameRefuseName`.
Changed `HSD_SisLib_803A74F0` to take a `GXColor*` instead of `u8*` and updated its implementation to access `color_rgb->r`, `color_rgb->g`, and `color_rgb->b`.
Removed many explicit `(u8*)` casts at `HSD_SisLib_803A74F0` call sites in `gmresult.c`, `mnname.c`, and newly added `mnnamenew.c` code.

Included broad formatting/include/order cleanup in files such as `ft_0D31.c`, `grcastle.c`, `toy.c`, item files, and library files; the strongest evidence is diff formatting changes rather than functional intent.
Bot report for GALE01 showed matched code rose to 61.33%, a +0.04% improvement and +1712 matched bytes.
smart_moves: Recovering `NameNewEntry` and `GlyphVariantEntry` made many offsets meaningful and enabled function prototypes to move away from `UNK_PARAMS`, which should reduce future type guessing in `mnnamenew`.
Splitting the large data blob at `.data:0x803EE35C` into named string symbols gave the compiler/decompilation a more accurate data layout and made asset-name references searchable.
Using `GXColor*` for `HSD_SisLib_803A74F0` encoded the actual API intent and removed brittle byte-pointer casts at call sites.

The implementation reused existing menu/name services (`GetNameCount`, `IsNameUnique`, `IsNameNotAllowed`, `CreateNameAtIndex`, `GetPersistentNameData`, `GetRumbleSettingOfPort`) instead of duplicating storage logic.
Name-entry input logic was decomposed into purpose-specific helpers for selection movement, auto-name picking, glyph variants, name validation, display refresh, and final writeback.

The bot-reported full matches on small helpers (`mnNameNew_8023BAA8`, `fn_8023CFC8`, `mnNameNew_8023B0F8`, `mnNameNew_8023B314`, `InitNameEntryUIState`) suggest the author stabilized leaf/control helpers before pushing larger still-partial functions.
decomp_lessons: When a UI unit uses many string asset names, split the `.data` string region into per-string symbols before or alongside code work; it improves evidence for archive lookups and reduces anonymous label churn.

Converting opaque user-data offsets into a struct can unlock a large cluster of functions at once, especially for menu state machines with repeated fields like JObj arrays, cursor state, selected mode, and text handles.
If a function semantically operates on a color struct, typing the API as `GXColor*` can remove casts and make both caller and callee cleaner without changing behavior.
For name/tag code, remember that display slots may be fixed-width character cells while final validation flattens multiple cell strings into one buffer before checking uniqueness/refusal lists.

Bot matching reports are useful for prioritizing follow-up: this PR fully matched several helpers but left major functions such as `mnNameNew_MainInput`, `mnNameNew_KeySetup`, and `mnNameNew_GlyphVariantSetup` as improved but not fully matched.
Large decomp PRs may include formatting-only churn in unrelated files; future reviewers/agents should separate core matching changes from style/prototype fallout when summarizing.
naming_conventions: Menu Name New functions use the `mnNameNew_` prefix plus address suffix when semantics are not yet known, e.g. `mnNameNew_8023BAA8` and `mnNameNew_8023CE4C`.

Recovered semantic helper names use PascalCase-style verbs for clear behaviors: `PickAutoName`, `NameContainsOnlySpaces`, `WriteCharactersForNameAtIndex`, `AddCharacterToName`, and `InitNameEntryUIState`.
Name-entry struct names introduced here are `NameNewEntry` and `GlyphVariantEntry`.
Data symbols follow `mnNameNew_<address>` for unidentified globals and strings, e.g. `mnNameNew_803EE35C`.
Castle stage static data in touched formatting code uses the `grCs_` prefix with address suffixes, e.g. `grCs_803B7EC8`.
Typed text/color API names retained the existing baselib address-style function name `HSD_SisLib_803A74F0` while improving the parameter type.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK` and `FORCE_PAD_STACK` in `mnnamenew.c` functions such as `mnNameNew_8023B0F8`, `PickAutoName`, `mnNameNew_GlyphVariantInput`, and `mnNameNew_MainInput` to influence stack layout for matching.
Preserved some compiler-shape artifacts such as temporary variables, address casts, and unusual expressions in the new code, indicating matching-driven C rather than purely idiomatic rewrite.
Broke a former single `lbl_803EE35C` data symbol into exact string-sized symbols in `symbols.txt`, a data-layout tactic that supports `.data` matching.
Kept address-suffixed names for unresolved functions/data while adding semantics only where behavior was clear enough.
Introduced a small inline helper `AddCharacterToName_getGlyphs` around glyph row access, likely to guide codegen for table indexing.
Changed `HSD_SisLib_803A74F0` call sites from `(u8*) &color` to `&color` after typing the API as `GXColor*`, reducing cast noise while preserving generated accesses through `.r/.g/.b`.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report showing 5 new matches and 20 unmatched-item improvements for `main/melee/mn/mnnamenew`.
Bot-reported new full matches: `mnNameNew_8023BAA8` (+676 bytes), `fn_8023CFC8` (+304), `mnNameNew_8023B0F8` (+300), `mnNameNew_8023B314` (+232), and `InitNameEntryUIState` (+200).

Bot-reported largest partial improvements included `mnNameNew_MainInput` (+1558, 67.63%), `mnNameNew_KeySetup` (+1468, 86.00%), `mnNameNew_GlyphVariantSetup` (+1439, 91.79%), and `fn_8023DBE8` (+1212, 95.90%).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `mnNameNew_MainInput`, `mnNameNew_KeySetup`, or `mnNameNew_GlyphVariantSetup`.
Search for later renames of `mnNameNew_8023BAA8`, `mnNameNew_8023CE4C`, `fn_8023CFC8`, and other address-suffixed helpers introduced or improved here.
Check whether `NameNewEntry` and `GlyphVariantEntry` fields were renamed after more of the name-entry UI was understood.
Audit all `HSD_SisLib_803A74F0` call sites for consistent `GXColor*` usage after this PR.
Look for PRs that further split or type `mnNameNew_803EDA7C`, `mnNameNew_803EDCE4`, and related glyph/string tables.
